points.text: Kymera Therapeutics announced Gilead Sciences exercised an option to license KT-200, an oral CDK2 molecular glue degrader, for development.

Kymera Therapeutics presented BroADen Phase 1b data for KT-621, an oral STAT6 degrader, at the American Academy of Dermatology Annual Meeting, showing consistent biological and clinical impact in atopic dermatitis.
Kymera Therapeutics — KT-200 — CDK2 Molecular Glue Degrader — Gilead Sciences exercised option to license program; deal includes upfront payment and potential milestone payments.
Kymera Therapeutics — KT-200 — CDK2 inhibition (molecular glue degrader) — Gilead Sciences exercised license option for development candidate.

Kymera Therapeutics — KT-621 — Atopic Dermatitis — BroADen Phase 1b single-arm trial presented at AAD 2026 showing consistent biomarker and clinical activity; Phase 2b BROADEN2 and BREADTH trials ongoing with data expected mid-2027 and late-2027.
Kymera Therapeutics — KT-200 — CDK2 Molecular Glue Degrader — Gilead Sciences exercised license option; program classified as molecular glue degrader targeting CDK2 for oncology applications.

Kymera Therapeutics — Advancing oral small molecule degrader medicines for immunological diseases — KT-200 (CDK2 molecular glue) + KT-621 (STAT6 degrader) — Gilead option exercised for KT-200; Phase 2b trials for KT-621 ongoing.

Kymera Therapeutics — Molecular glue degrader platform expansion — KT-200 (CDK2 degrader) — Gilead option exercised; program leverages proprietary molecular glue technology for targeted protein degradation.

KT-200 — Mechanism: Oral CDK2 molecular glue degrader — Phase: Preclinical/Development candidate selection — Population: Not specified — Comparator: N/A — Status: Licensed by Gilead Sciences for development.

KT-621 — Mechanism: Oral STAT6 degrader (first-in-class) — Phase: 1b (BroADen) — Population: Atopic dermatitis — Comparator: N/A (single-arm) — Results: Consistent impact on biomarkers and clinical measures presented at AAD 2026.

KT-200 — Mechanism: Molecular glue-mediated CDK2 degradation — Phase: Preclinical/Early clinical (implied by license exercise) — Population: Not specified in source — Comparator: Not specified — Endpoints: License option exercise by Gilead confirms mechanistic validation of molecular glue approach.
Kymera Therapeutics / Gilead Sciences — License option exercise — KT-200 (CDK2 molecular glue degrader) — Gilead exercised option to license development candidate; financial terms undisclosed.
Kymera Therapeutics / Gilead Sciences — License option exercise — KT-200 (CDK2 molecular glue degrader) — Gilead exercised option; deal includes undisclosed upfront and potential milestone payments.
